etymology.description: semantic 髟(髟) (biāo, "hair") + phonetic 冉(冉) (rǎn, "gradual") -> "beard"
etymology.interpretation: The character 髯(rán, "beard") uses the semantic component 髟(髟) (biāo, "hair") to denote its relation to hair, specifically facial hair. The phonetic component 冉(冉) (rǎn, "gradual") contributes the sound. A beard, consisting of hair on the chin and face, aligns with the imagery and meaning of these combined components.

examples.chinese: 他的长髯非常抢眼(他的長髯非常搶眼)。
examples.english: His long beard is very striking.

examples.chinese: 这位老人有一副白髯(這位老人有一副白髯)，看起来很有智慧(看起來很有智慧)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè wèi lǎorén yǒu yī fù báirán, kàn qǐlái hěn yǒu zhìhuì.
examples.english: The old man has a white beard, which makes him look very wise.

examples.chinese: 小说中的角色常常拥有长髯(小說中的角色常常擁有長髯)，增添了神秘感(增添了神秘感)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎoshuō zhōng de juésè chángcháng yǒngyǒu chángrán, zēngtiān le shénmì gǎn.
examples.english: Characters in novels often have long beards, adding a sense of mystery.
